promotions.label: Evaluating and Optimizing JITAIs in a Digital Mental Health Intervention (Wysa)
promotions.reason: Directly applicable JITAI methodology: addresses evaluation and optimization of JITAIs, which is the core framework of this project. The methods for assessing JITAI effectiveness and using data to improve intervention timing/delivery transfer directly to physical activity JITAI context, even though the domain (mental health) differs.

promotions.label: Multimodal Personalized Mobile Health JITAI for Occupational Stress Management
promotions.reason: Directly applicable JITAI methodology: implements a multimodal personalized JITAI with mobile sensing and adaptive intervention delivery. The personalization framework and multimodal data integration strategies are directly relevant to the project's config-driven RL approach for physical activity JITAIs.

promotions.label: Extended COM-B model for digital health: incorporating technology affordances
promotions.reason: Theoretical foundation directly relevant to the project: COM-B is a primary behavioral framework underpinning JITAI design, and this paper extends it specifically for digital health contexts. The COM-B model is explicitly listed as relevant theoretical foundation for behavioral intervention design in the project.

promotions.label: Reliability and validity of commercially available wearable devices for measuring step count and HR
promotions.reason: Core domain data: validates consumer wearable step count and heart rate accuracy (Apple, Samsung, Fitbit, Garmin). Directly informs the project's sensor pipeline for step_bin state estimation and activity measurement, establishing which devices provide reliable PA data.

promotions.label: Comparison of raw accelerometry data from ActiGraph, Apple Watch, Garmin, and Fitbit
promotions.reason: Core domain data on accelerometer/PPG processing: compares raw accelerometry data formats and quality across research-grade and consumer devices. Directly relevant to the project's sensor processing needs for extracting state features from wearable accelerometer data.

promotions.label: Heart rate measurement accuracy of Fitbit Charge 4 and Samsung Galaxy Watch
promotions.reason: Core domain data on wearable PPG processing: quantifies HR measurement accuracy vs ECG reference for consumer wearables. The MAE findings inform the project's understanding of sensor noise in PPG-based heart rate data used for state estimation.

promotions.label: Heart rate detection by Fitbit ChargeHR validation study
promotions.reason: Core domain data on wearable HR accuracy during sleep: Fitbit HR validation against polysomnography. Relevant for the project's sleep state dimension and understanding sensor performance during sedentary/low-activity periods.

promotions.label: Performance of a commercial multi-sensor wearable (Fitbit Charge HR) in measuring sleep and steps
promotions.reason: Core domain data on wearable multi-sensor accuracy: validates step count and sleep measurement in children. Directly relevant to the project's step_bin and sleep state variables derived from wearable sensors.

promotions.label: Fitbit's accuracy to measure short bouts of stepping and sedentary behaviour
promotions.reason: Core domain data on step detection and sedentary behavior classification: validates Fitbit's sensitivity for short stepping bouts and specificity for sedentary periods. Directly relevant to the project's step_bin state variable and activity classification needs.

promotions.label: Understanding activity and physiology at scale: the Apple Heart & Movement Study
promotions.reason: Core domain data on large-scale physical activity data collection: demonstrates scalable collection of HealthKit activity, heart rate, and movement data from 200,000+ participants. Directly relevant to understanding wearable PA data characteristics at population scale.

promotions.label: Validity and reliability of the Apple Health app on iPhone for measuring gait parameters
promotions.reason: Core domain data on consumer device step count and gait measurement: validates Apple Health app step count, walking distance, and gait speed. Relevant for projects using iOS/HealthKit-based step data for PA state estimation.

promotions.label: Validation of Garmin and Polar devices for continuous heart rate monitoring during exercise
promotions.reason: Core domain data on wearable HR validation during physical activity: quantifies Garmin/Polar HR accuracy at different exercise intensities. Directly relevant for understanding PPG sensor performance during PA, the core measurement domain of the project.

promotions.label: Continuous monitoring of heart rate variability in free-living conditions using Garmin
promotions.reason: Core domain data on wearable HRV monitoring: validates Garmin HRV data in free-living conditions and documents API data retrieval characteristics. Relevant for the project's sensor pipeline and understanding wearable data availability patterns.

promotions.label: Limiting racial disparities and bias for wearable devices in health science research
promotions.reason: Core domain data on wearable sensor bias: documents systematic PPG measurement bias across skin tone, BMI, and age groups. Directly relevant for ensuring equitable PA measurement in the project's state estimation and avoiding biased RL policies.

promotions.label: Wearable sensors with possibilities for data exchange: analyzing status and needs
promotions.reason: Implementation-relevant: analyzes wearable API access for research (Fitbit, Garmin, Apple HealthKit, Google Fit). Documents data availability, rate limits, and integration patterns that directly inform the project's sensor data ingestion pipeline design.

promotions.label: Challenges and recommendations for wearable devices in digital health: data quality
promotions.reason: Core domain data on wearable data quality: systematizes four major challenges (data quality, interoperability, demographic bias, security) for consumer wearables. Directly relevant to understanding data quality issues in the project's wearable sensor stream.

promotions.label: Data quality evaluation in wearable monitoring
promotions.reason: Core domain data on wearable sensor data quality: proposes a standardized framework for evaluating wearable data quality and quantifies motion artifact rates. Directly applicable to the project's sensor processing pipeline for assessing and filtering noisy wearable data.
